049f9.summary: Model-first editor core with abstract shapes and bloc state
049f9.content: The package is currently model-first. It exposes an abstract GridShape contract for future non-rectangular grid-snappable shapes, a GridRectangleShape MVP implementation, an immutable GridLayout aggregate for serialization, and a GridEditorBloc for event-driven editing state.

078b8.summary: Separate editor widgets from bloc, models, and internal helpers
078b8.content: The public package API now keeps rendering concerns in dedicated widget files. GridEditor hosts bloc binding, GridCanvas renders layout state, and GridRectangleTile renders individual rectangles while snapping math and JSON helpers stay in dedicated internal files.

097f5.summary: Ship a routed example app for package demos
097f5.content: The repository now ships a routed Flutter example app under example/ with a landing page and separate demo pages for fixed shapes, constrained shapes, and free-flowing layered shapes. The example app is split into app, catalog, page, widget, and layout seed files so examples stay maintainable as the package grows.

0bb9c.summary: Translate editor gestures into bloc move and resize events
0bb9c.content: The editor widget layer now supports direct manipulation. Rectangle tiles can be dragged to snapped grid points, and selected editable tiles expose a dedicated resize handle that resizes through snapped grid sizes while leaving fixed shapes locked.

102a5.summary: Model supports frozen and fixed-size movable non-overlapping shapes
102a5.content: The grid model now distinguishes fully frozen shapes from fixed-size movable shapes. Frozen shapes lock both position and size. Fixed-size movable shapes may move across snapped grid points but stay non-overlapping and keep their snapped size. Any move or resize that would overlap a non-overlapping shape restores the prior valid layout state.

1227c.summary: Clamp editor interactions and rendering to visible board bounds
1227c.content: The canvas layer now clamps rendered rectangles, drag destinations, and resize results to the visible board capacity derived from the current viewport and snap cell size. Shapes no longer render or move beyond the board edges even when a layout loads with out-of-range positions or a drag attempts to leave the canvas.

14799.summary: Normalize loaded layouts and keyboard nudges through board clamp rules
14799.content: The editor now applies the same board-bound clamp rules to imported layouts, keyboard nudges, pointer dragging, resizing, and rendering. GridEditor computes the visible board size, normalizes any loaded layout back into bounds, and reuses the same snap-based clamp helpers before dispatching keyboard movement so all movement channels converge on one boundary contract.

16850.summary: Use a dictionary helper as a thin persistence boundary
16850.content: The package now exposes a helper that converts simple dictionaries keyed by shape id into typed GridLayout and GridShape runtime objects, and back again. This keeps the live editor on typed models for movement, overlap, clamping, and serialization rules while still letting callers load and save plain map data when they want a simpler integration surface.
